name: Participant Flow Strategy
consequences: This lever governs the balance between participant recruitment speed and participant quality. Direct effect: faster recruitment might compromise selection criteria, while slower recruitment ensures a more curated cohort.
options: Optimize for high-volume participant intake through aggressive marketing campaigns
Prioritize participant quality over quantity by implementing stricter inclusion/exclusion criteria
Employ a hybrid approach combining targeted recruitment with selective screening to strike a balance between speed and quality
review: This lever overlooks the impact of participant dropout rates on data yield.

name: Sensing Tier Strategy
consequences: This lever controls the trade-off between data resolution and participant comfort. Direct effect: escalating to enhanced nights can disrupt natural sleep patterns, while maintaining low-burden monitoring might compromise data quality.
options: Maintain a tiered sensing model with gradual escalation to full PSG only in response to technician-flagged events
Deploy full PSG on all nights for enhanced resolution at the cost of participant comfort and potential data saturation
Implement an adaptive sensing strategy that adjusts the level of detail based on individual participant characteristics
review: This lever misses the downstream implications of sensor saturation on event-triage algorithm performance.

name: Staffing Model Optimization
consequences: This lever dictates the balance between staffing costs and operational efficiency. Direct effect: hiring additional research technicians can improve coverage and response times, while maintaining a lean staff might compromise participant safety.
options: Maintain a core team of experienced research technicians with flexible scheduling to ensure adequate coverage and event response
Hire additional research technicians to reduce the workload on existing staff and enhance operational efficiency
Implement an on-call system for senior staff members to provide backup support during critical periods
review: This lever overlooks the impact of staffing ratios on participant safety and data quality.

name: Data Sharing Protocol
consequences: This lever controls access to sensitive participant data. Direct effect: public sharing can promote collaboration and accelerate discovery, while restrictive protocols might limit data utilization.
options: Implement a strict permission-based system for accessing de-identified physiological data with controlled public sharing through recognized repositories
Employ an open-data policy allowing unrestricted access to all de-identified data in exchange for participant consent
Adopt a tiered data-sharing model where sensitive information is shared only within the research community
review: This lever misses the tension between data sharing and participant privacy.

name: Event-Triage Algorithm Development
consequences: This lever governs the balance between algorithm performance and manual review burden. Direct effect: more complex algorithms can improve accuracy but increase reliance on human scorers.
options: Develop a semi-automated event-triage model with adjustable thresholds for probable parasomnia episodes to reduce manual review burden
Implement an entirely automated event-triage system that prioritizes speed over accuracy and relies heavily on machine learning
Employ a hybrid approach combining automated triage with real-time human oversight to balance performance and reliability
review: This lever overlooks the impact of algorithm performance on participant safety.

name: Budget Reallocation Strategy
consequences: This lever dictates the trade-off between budget allocation for personnel costs versus equipment and participant compensation. Direct effect: prioritizing staff salaries can ensure safe operations but compromise data quality.
options: Allocate a significant portion of the budget to equipment upgrades and participant compensation to enhance data resolution and participant comfort
Prioritize staffing costs over equipment and participant compensation to maintain safe operational standards and efficient data collection
Implement a flexible budget reallocation strategy allowing for dynamic adjustments based on project progress and needs
review: This lever misses the tension between budget allocation and participant safety.

name: Scientific Advisory Board Composition
consequences: This lever governs the balance between external expertise and internal operational control. Direct effect: involving more external advisors can provide diverse perspectives but compromise operational authority.
options: Maintain a small, tightly-knit scientific advisory board with direct access to the PI for operational decisions
Expand the advisory board to include additional external experts from various fields to enhance diversity of thought
Implement an alternating membership model where external advisors serve staggered terms to maintain a balance between expertise and operational control
review: This lever overlooks the impact of advisory board composition on project governance.

name: Participant Recruitment Diversification
consequences: Diversifying recruitment channels will increase participant enrollment but may lead to a higher proportion of participants with comorbidities, requiring additional exclusions and potentially altering the study's power.
options: Expand recruitment through online forums and social media platforms targeting sleep disorder communities
Establish partnerships with local sleep clinics and private practices to refer patients directly into the study
Implement a participant ambassador program where successful completers promote the study within their networks
review: This lever governs the tension between participant diversity and protocol purity, but the options overlook potential biases in online recruitment.

name: Sensor Data Quality Control
consequences: Implementing rigorous data quality control measures will ensure high-quality sensor data but may introduce additional costs and logistical complexities.
options: Develop and implement automated data cleaning pipelines to detect and correct errors
Establish a dedicated data quality team to manually review and verify all sensor data
Integrate data validation checks directly into the sensing tier's real-time monitoring system
review: Prioritizing data quality over cost carries hidden costs: none of the options address potential equipment obsolescence or software compatibility issues.

name: Participant Engagement and Retention
consequences: Improving participant engagement and retention will enhance study outcomes but may compromise the naturalistic environment required for accurate parasomnia capture.
options: Implement a participant feedback and suggestions system to foster a sense of ownership and investment in the study
Offer personalized support and counseling services to address participants' emotional and psychological needs during their stay
Provide participants with real-time access to their own sensor data and sleep metrics for educational and engagement purposes
review: This lever governs the tension between participant experience and study fidelity, but the options overlook potential risks of participant manipulation or bias.

name: Neuroscientific Advisory Board Composition
consequences: Assembling a diverse neuroscientific advisory board will provide valuable expertise and guidance but may introduce additional governance complexities.
options: Recruit experts in sleep neurophysiology, computational neuroscience, and machine learning to inform algorithm development
Establish partnerships with leading research institutions and universities to tap into their expertise and resources
Invite industry representatives from pharmaceutical companies specializing in sleep disorders to provide commercial insights
review: Prioritizing scientific credibility over governance simplicity carries hidden costs: none of the options address potential conflicts of interest or intellectual property disputes.

name: Data Sharing and Publication
consequences: Fostering a culture of open data sharing will accelerate scientific progress but may compromise participant privacy and confidentiality.
options: Deposit de-identified physiological datasets into public repositories for broad access and reuse
Establish partnerships with leading research institutions to create collaborative, multi-institutional data platforms
Implement a tiered data sharing model where sensitive information is shared only with authorized researchers or collaborators
review: This lever governs the tension between scientific progress and participant privacy, but the options overlook potential risks of unauthorized data reuse or misinterpretation.

name: Expansion Criteria Definition
consequences: Defining expansion criteria will directly affect the facility's operational capacity, influencing participant enrollment and event capture rates. Downstream implications include potential changes to staffing ratios, sensor deployment strategies, or data analysis pipelines.
options: Establish a tiered participant recruitment model with escalating compensation levels to incentivize participation
Develop a probabilistic expansion framework based on historical participant engagement patterns and sensor yield
Implement a rolling review process for the participant selection criteria to adapt to emerging trends in parasomnia research
review: This lever governs the tension between scalability and operational control, but the options overlook the impact of expansion on local regulatory compliance.
